findings.description: Three artifact families have create-once protocol in architecture.json and schemas but are actually multi-writer read-modify-write. Same class as the draft.md issue fixed in cycle 2, but these were missed. (1) characters/index.json: protagonist-profiler creates it, supporting-cast-developer says 'Update characters/index.json with new entries'. (2) characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json: character-voice-designer says 'Write updated characters/CHAR-NNN.json files with refined voiceFingerprint and sensorySig fields' — editing existing files. (3) chapter-outlines/{N}.json: chapter-outliner creates them, scene-beat-designer says 'Update chapter-outlines/{N}.json' — enriching existing files with beats.
findings.evidence: architecture.json: characters/index.json writeProtocol='create-once', writers=['protagonist-profiler','supporting-cast-developer']. characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json writeProtocol='create-once', writers=['protagonist-profiler','supporting-cast-developer','character-voice-designer']. chapter-outlines/{N}.json writeProtocol='create-once', writers=['chapter-outliner','scene-beat-designer']. Schema files confirm create-once labeling. Agent prompts describe update/edit operations on existing files. Rules skill defines create-once as 'Check that the file does NOT exist before writing. If it exists, you are in a retry' — agents editing existing files would misinterpret the file's existence as a retry signal.
findings.recommendation: Change architecture.json and corresponding schema files to read-modify-write protocol for all three artifact families. Add multi-writer ordering rules (same pattern used for the draft.md fix). For characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json, note that profiler/cast-developer write DIFFERENT files (valid create-once per-file) but voice-designer edits ALL existing files — consider split protocol: create-once for initial creation, read-modify-write for voice refinement pass.

findings.description: Three agents perform read-modify-write operations on artifacts declared as create-once, violating the write protocol contract. Direct consequence of AF-ARTIFACT-02.
findings.evidence: supporting-cast-developer prompt L48: 'Update characters/index.json with new entries'. character-voice-designer prompt L48: 'Write updated characters/CHAR-NNN.json files with refined voiceFingerprint and sensorySig fields'. scene-beat-designer prompt step 6: 'Update chapter-outlines/{N}.json'. All three artifacts are labeled create-once in architecture.json and their schema files.
findings.recommendation: Fix jointly with AF-ARTIFACT-02. Update write protocols in architecture.json, schemas, and consider adding sequential ordering specification as done for draft.md.

findings.agent: 21 pure routers (orchestrator + 8 coordinators + 10 sub-coordinators + guide + orchestrator)
findings.description: Pure routers do not reference manifest.json prepend. By design — they produce no creative artifacts, only status.json. Carried from cycle 0.
findings.evidence: manifest.schema.md says 'Every agent prepends an entry after completing work.' 21 router agents have no manifest prepend instructions. This is architecturally correct — manifest tracks creative output, not routing decisions.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. Document the design decision in manifest.schema.md: 'Pure routers (coordinators, sub-coordinators, orchestrator, guide) do not prepend manifest entries as they produce no creative artifacts.'

findings.description: revision-loop is declared as a result code for all 8 phase coordinators and 10 sub-coordinators, but NO coordinator's routing table contains a code path that produces it. Every coordinator can only write 'complete' or 'blocked'. The orchestrator has a routing rule for revision-loop ('Wait — coordinator is in its internal auditor retry loop') that can never trigger. This is dead routing code across the entire system.
findings.evidence: Exhaustive check of all 19 coordinator routing tables in routing.json: every coordinator's achievable output set is {'complete', 'blocked'}. No routing rule action contains 'write own status: revision-loop'. The orchestrator routing table at row 25 handles revision-loop but no agent can produce it. Roster.json declares revision-loop for all 8 phase coordinators.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add a code path: when a coordinator is in its retry loop (retries > 0, retries < max), write status revision-loop before re-dispatching writers, so the orchestrator can observe retry-in-progress state; or (b) remove revision-loop from all coordinator result codes and remove the orchestrator routing rule. Option (a) is semantically richer and enables progress monitoring.

findings.description: Orchestrator dispatches 3 specialists directly: continuity-tracker, series-kb-manager, craft-tracker. These are cross-cutting pipeline boundary agents, not part of any creative phase. Architecturally justified. Carried from cycle 0.
findings.evidence: Roster: orchestrator children include continuity-tracker (specialist), series-kb-manager (specialist), craft-tracker (specialist). These agents operate at pipeline transitions (between phases), not within any coordinator's creative phase.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. The cross-cutting pattern is standard for pipeline boundary agents.

findings.description: 6 adversarial beta-path agents completely lack Anti-Laziness sections. Beta readers are the adversarial review layer for published prose; the synthesizer makes the revision/approve decision. Without anti-laziness enforcement, these agents could rubber-stamp chapters.
findings.evidence: Searched all 6 agent prompts for 'Anti-Laziness', 'anti-laziness', 'anti laziness' — zero matches. No section header, no inline reference. Compare with auditors which all have Anti-Laziness sections. The beta readers are adversarial agents with quality-gate responsibilities equal to auditors.
findings.recommendation: Add Anti-Laziness Rules section to all 6 beta-path agents. Include: (1) item-by-item verification — every scene must be independently evaluated, (2) evidence requirement — cite specific passages, (3) zero-findings suspicion clause — 'If your first pass finds zero issues in a full chapter, that is suspicious. Re-read.'

findings.description: 4 of 8 auditors have Anti-Laziness sections but are missing the zero-findings suspicion clause. Without it, these auditors can return 'passed' after a cursory review. Additionally, 5 of 8 auditors lack explicit evidence requirements (cite specific artifacts/passages).
findings.evidence: character-auditor: has Anti-Laziness section, has item-by-item, missing zero-suspicion, missing evidence. plotting-auditor: has Anti-Laziness section, has item-by-item, missing zero-suspicion, missing evidence. style-auditor: has Anti-Laziness section, missing item-by-item, missing zero-suspicion, missing evidence. revision-auditor: has Anti-Laziness section, has item-by-item, missing zero-suspicion, has evidence. worldbuilding-auditor: has Anti-Laziness section, has zero-suspicion, missing evidence, missing item-by-item.
findings.recommendation: Standardize all 8 auditor Anti-Laziness sections to include all four components: (1) Anti-Laziness section header, (2) item-by-item verification, (3) 'cite specific evidence' requirement, (4) 'If zero findings on first pass, that is suspicious' clause. Consider a shared anti-laziness skill that all auditors reference.

findings.description: Specialists have no retry awareness mechanism. When a coordinator retries (auditor failed → reset → re-dispatch), specialists re-run from scratch with no access to what the auditor rejected. The coordinator deletes their status and re-dispatches them, but specialists don't read gate files (audit-reports/{phase}/gate.json) and have no 'Step 0: Check for Retry' process. They would produce identical output on retry because they see the same inputs.
findings.evidence: Checked all 37 non-auditor specialist prompts: 0/37 reference gate.json or audit-report files in their Reads section. 8/37 have incidental retry-adjacent text (iteration references from skills) but no direct gate-file consumption. The coordinator-patterns skill says 'writers MUST read this on retry' (line 28) referring to audit-reports/{phase}/gate.json, but no specialist prompt implements this instruction.
findings.recommendation: Add to every specialist that participates in auditor retry loops: (1) add audit-reports/{phase}/gate.json to Reads section, (2) add 'Step 0: Check for retry — if gate.json exists for this phase, read it and incorporate feedback into your work' as the first process step. This affects ~29 specialists (excluding delivery-assembler, summary-generator, series-kb-manager, and other non-gated agents).

resolved: AF-CONTRACT-02 (skill result code table) — FIXED via gap CR-003. Table now correctly attributes revision-required to beta-synthesizer data field and revision-loop to all coordinators.
AF-ARTIFACT-01 (phantom plot-architect reference) — FIXED via gap CR-002. No remaining references to romantic-fantasy-writer-plot-architect in any file.
partiallyResolved: AF-ARTIFACT-02 (create-once protocol mismatch) — draft.md FIXED via gap AC-005, but 3 additional artifact families (characters/index.json, characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json, chapter-outlines/{N}.json) have the same issue. These were not in scope for cycle 2 gap fixes.
AF-ARTIFACT-04 (create-once violation) — draft.md FIXED, but same 3 new instances remain.
FW-LAZY-04 (auditor zero-findings gaps) — All 8 auditors now have Anti-Laziness sections (improvement from cycle 1). But 4 still lack zero-findings suspicion clause.
unresolved: FW-PURITY-03 (revision-loop dead path) — Unchanged. No coordinator routing table can produce revision-loop.
FW-LAZY-01 (beta path anti-laziness) — Unchanged. 6 beta-path agents lack Anti-Laziness sections entirely.
FW-ADDITIONAL-01 (specialist retry feedback gap) — Unchanged. 0/37 specialists reference gate files.
carriedDesignDecisions: AF-MANIFEST-01 (INFO) — Pure routers lack manifest. By design.
FW-HIERARCHY-01 (INFO) — Orchestrator dispatches 3 cross-cutting specialists. By design.

AC-004.evidence: craft-profile.schema.md now correctly declares create-once protocol with sole writer romantic-fantasy-writer-craft-profile-selector. The reference to plot-architect as a secondary writer is removed. The schema explicitly states: 'Once written, craft-profile.json is immutable — selected tools become binding invariants (INV-069, INV-079).'

AC-005.evidence: chapters.schema.md now declares read-modify-write protocol for draft.md with 4 sequential writers, draftVersion frontmatter field, and explicit multi-writer ordering rules. This matches the roster writer declarations.

CR-002.evidence: grep for 'plot-architect' across all agents/, schemas/, and skills/ returns zero matches. The phantom agent reference is completely removed.

CR-003.evidence: agent-as-function-contract/SKILL.md result code table now correctly notes revision-required as 'beta-synthesizer (data field in synthesis output, surfaced by beta-reading-coordinator)' and revision-loop as 'All phase coordinators (8) — orchestrator consumes this code for cross-phase routing'.

DC-W05.evidence: README.md now shows P0=7, P1=16, P2=3 matching the actual test directory contents. 7 P0 scenarios listed by ID in the README table match TEST-001 through TEST-022 P0 designations.

MK-W03.evidence: grep for 'Ralph' and 'ralph' across all skills/ files returns zero matches. The mcp-sidecar/SKILL.md external reference to 'Ralph Orchestrator' has been removed. File has 109 lines with no external references.

summary.newIssues: AF-ARTIFACT-02 now expanded to cover 3 additional artifact families (characters/index.json, characters/{CHAR-NNN}.json, chapter-outlines/{N}.json) not addressed in cycle 2 gap fixes. All 6 cycle-2 gap fixes verified from independent perspective. Remaining warnings are persisting architectural issues that were not targeted by gap hunting.
summary.architectureHealth: Good (B+). Sound pipeline architecture, consistent hierarchy, proper convergence bounds via skill indirection, comprehensive invariant coverage. Remaining issues affect documentation accuracy (write protocol labels), adversarial enforcement completeness (anti-laziness gaps), retry mechanism clarity (specialist gate awareness), and routing hygiene (dead revision-loop path). No issues threaten fundamental correctness.
